shortDescription.text: Server-Side Request Forgery via user-controlled URLs
Insufficient server-side validation of uploaded file type/content
Missing or insufficient input validation before use
Resource exhaustion via unbounded input processing
Unauthorized access to resources via unvalidated identifiers

message.text: website_crawl URLs in the indexing estimate endpoint are passed directly to WebsiteInfo without scheme, host, or format validation. An authenticated user can supply arbitrary URLs (including internal IPs, cloud metadata endpoints, or file:// URIs) that will be forwarded to the downstream crawl service. The info_list field is typed as dict[str, Any] with no Pydantic schema enforcement on nested structures.

Firecrawl and Watercrawl auth services use a user-configurable base_url from stored credentials to build HTTP request targets. The requests are made via httpx.post() directly—not through ssrf_proxy. An attacker who configures a credential with base_url pointing to internal services (e.g., http://169.254.169.254) can perform SSRF against cloud metadata endpoints or internal infrastructure.

DocumentAddByFileApi and DocumentUpdateByFileApi pass the client-supplied file.mimetype directly to FileService.upload_file() without server-side magic-byte validation at the controller layer. The downstream ExtractProcessor selects the parser based on file extension (suffix), not re-verified content type. An attacker can upload a polyglot file with a benign extension but malicious content, or manipulate the MIME type header to bypass type restrictions.

DocumentAddByFileApi and DocumentUpdateByFileApi parse request.form['data'] via json.loads() into a raw dict, then access nested fields (embedding_model_provider, retrieval_model.reranking_model) before Pydantic validation via KnowledgeConfig.model_validate(). Malformed JSON structures (e.g., string instead of dict for retrieval_model) cause unhandled TypeError/AttributeError crashes. Additionally, doc_form is validated via Pydantic in the text-based creation path but not in the file-based path before KnowledgeConfig construction, creating an asymmetric validation gap.

DocumentAddByFileApi calls file.read() to load the entire uploaded file into memory before passing it to FileService.upload_file(). No file size check occurs at the controller layer before memory allocation. Unlike DocumentUpdateByFileApi which catches FileTooLargeError, the create path does not, allowing arbitrarily large uploads to exhaust server memory before any size validation occurs.

The indexing estimate endpoint accepts Notion import parameters (workspace_id, credential_id, page_id) as unvalidated nested dicts within info_list (typed as dict[str, Any]). An authenticated user can supply arbitrary credential_id values belonging to other workspaces to probe or use Notion credentials they should not have access to, depending on how NotionExtractor scopes credential lookups.

ExtractProcessor.load_from_url() derives the file suffix from the attacker-controlled URL via Path(url).suffix, and falls back to the Content-Disposition header filename. While the HTTP request goes through ssrf_proxy (partial mitigation), the attacker controls which extractor is selected by manipulating the URL or response headers. A URL ending in .xml steers parsing to UnstructuredXmlExtractor, which may be vulnerable to XXE if external entity processing is not disabled in the underlying library.
